_note: Fixture corpus for offline dev. ~12 representative GLP-1 + peptide therapeutics papers with synthesized abstracts. Real corpus comes from `pnpm --filter @novamind/corpus ingest`. Do not cite these PMIDs in any production output.

papers.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for the treatment of obesity (SURMOUNT-1)
papers.abstract: Tirzepatide, a dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist, was evaluated in adults with obesity over 72 weeks. Treatment produced substantial and sustained body-weight reduction relative to placebo across all dose arms, with gastrointestinal adverse events as the most common side effect.

papers.title: Semaglutide for cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes (SUSTAIN-6)
papers.abstract: Once-weekly semaglutide reduced major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with type 2 diabetes at high cardiovascular risk over a median follow-up of approximately two years. The benefit appeared driven primarily by a reduction in nonfatal stroke.

papers.title: Once-weekly semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity (STEP-1)
papers.abstract: STEP-1 evaluated subcutaneous semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly versus placebo for 68 weeks in adults with overweight or obesity. Mean body-weight reduction in the semaglutide arm was substantially greater than placebo, with gastrointestinal events common but typically mild and transient.

papers.title: Triple agonist retatrutide for chronic weight management — phase 2
papers.abstract: Retatrutide, an agonist of the GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors, was assessed in a phase 2 trial in adults with obesity. Higher dose arms produced mean weight reduction exceeding twenty-four percent at 48 weeks, with adverse events consistent with the incretin class.

papers.title: Tirzepatide versus semaglutide once weekly in type 2 diabetes (SURPASS-2)
papers.abstract: Head-to-head comparison of tirzepatide versus semaglutide 1 mg weekly in patients with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled on metformin. Tirzepatide produced greater HbA1c reduction across dose arms, with similar gastrointestinal tolerability.

papers.title: Liraglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes (LEADER)
papers.abstract: Liraglutide reduced the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events versus placebo in patients with type 2 diabetes at high cardiovascular risk. Reductions in cardiovascular death and all-cause mortality were observed over a median 3.8-year follow-up.

papers.title: Mechanism of action of GLP-1 receptor agonists: pharmacology and clinical impact
papers.abstract: This review summarizes the pharmacology of GLP-1 receptor agonists, covering insulinotropic effects, glucagon suppression, gastric emptying, and central appetite regulation. Connections between molecular pharmacology and observed clinical outcomes in diabetes and obesity are discussed.

papers.title: Lipidation strategies for half-life extension of therapeutic peptides
papers.abstract: Fatty-acid lipidation enables albumin binding, prolonging in vivo half-life of GLP-1 analogs and other peptide therapeutics. The review covers chain-length effects, linker chemistries, and case studies of approved lipidated peptides including liraglutide and semaglutide.

papers.title: PEGylation in peptide therapeutics: progress and current limitations
papers.abstract: PEGylation extends circulating half-life of peptide drugs by reducing renal clearance and proteolytic degradation. The review discusses immunogenicity concerns, conjugation site selection, and regulatory considerations relative to alternative half-life extension strategies.

papers.title: Oral delivery of peptide therapeutics: SNAC and beyond
papers.abstract: Permeation enhancers including sodium N-(8-[2-hydroxybenzoyl]amino)caprylate (SNAC) enable oral bioavailability of peptide therapeutics, exemplified by oral semaglutide. The review addresses absorption mechanisms, formulation strategies, and remaining bioavailability ceiling.

papers.title: Cyclic peptides as therapeutics: design principles and clinical translation
papers.abstract: Cyclic peptides offer improved metabolic stability, conformational rigidity, and target selectivity over linear analogs. The review covers macrocyclization chemistries, screening platforms, and approved/clinical-stage examples spanning oncology, infectious disease, and metabolic indications.

papers.title: Peptide-drug conjugates for targeted oncology: design and clinical pipeline
papers.abstract: Peptide-drug conjugates combine receptor-targeting peptides with cytotoxic payloads to improve tumor selectivity. The review surveys linker chemistries, payload selection, and the late-stage clinical pipeline including somatostatin- and integrin-targeting agents.
